No Poverty.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description or objectives supports a contribution to eradicating poverty.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description or objectives relates to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description or objectives relates to health, well-being, or medical systems.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops analytical, problem-solving, and teamwork skills for employment, aligning with Target 4.4.
evidence: develop the competency and skills for analyzing and designing information systems
knowledgeable of analytical, problem-solving, and teamwork skills

Gender Equality.reason: While information systems can be enabling technology, the syllabus lacks explicit links to gender equality or women's empowerment (Target 5.b).
evidence: develop the competency and skills for analyzing and designing information systems

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description or objectives relates to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description or objectives relates to energy access or renewable energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly supports 'business operations,' 'business sustainability,' and 'business innovation,' contributing to economic productivity and technological upgrading (Target 8.2).
evidence: Profession of information technology and knowledge of business innovation

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's core focus is 'System Analysis and Design,' directly contributing to information systems as essential infrastructure and technological capabilities (Targets 9.1, 9.5, 9.c).
evidence: apply information systems to deliver digital and suitable solutions
Ability to manage and integrate information system

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description or objectives relates to reducing income or social inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description or objectives relates to urban planning or sustainable communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: While 'business sustainability' is mentioned, the course does not explicitly link to sustainable consumption, production patterns, or resource efficiency (Targets 12.2, 12.6).

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description or objectives relates to climate change mitigation or adaptation.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description or objectives relates to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description or objectives relates to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's focus on 'organizational management or governance' and 'digital solutions' can contribute to effective and transparent institutions (Target 16.6).
evidence: apply information systems to deliver digital and suitable solutions

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course fosters 'Communication, coordination abilities' (Core Competence C) and builds 'information technology' capacity, an enabling technology for development (Target 17.8).
evidence: Communication, coordination abilities and international capabilities
